message:
fix(browser): eliminate notify_ready race window and add Next.js bridge response unwrappers

Browser reactor notify_ready() had a TOCTOU race: it dropped the registrations
lock before acquiring the pending events lock, allowing a concurrent
modify()/deregister() to change interest between the two acquisitions. Fix by
holding the registrations lock through the pending queue insertion, matching
the lock order used by modify() and deregister().

Added notify_ready_with_barriers() test helper for deterministic concurrency
testing of the race window.

Next.js SDK (@asupersync/next) gains:
- "panicked" added to NextBridgeOutcome union type
- createNextServerBridgeResponseFromOutcome() and
  createNextEdgeBridgeResponseFromOutcome() — convert internal Outcome<T>
  to typed bridge responses with full diagnostics
- unwrapNextServerBridgeResponse() and unwrapNextEdgeBridgeResponse() —
  extract payload from successful responses or throw structured errors
- NextServerBridgeResponseError and NextEdgeBridgeResponseError types with
  dedicated error codes (ASUPERSYNC_NEXT_SERVER_BRIDGE_RESPONSE,
  ASUPERSYNC_NEXT_EDGE_BRIDGE_RESPONSE) and cloned diagnostics
- createNextServerBridgeResponseError() and
  createNextEdgeBridgeResponseError() internal factories

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
